info.title: DepVault API
info.description: DepVault platform API — dependency analysis, vulnerability scanning, and encrypted secret management across any tech stack

tags.name: Secrets
tags.description: Secret file storage and one-time sharing

bearerAuth.description: JWT access token obtained from /api/auth/login or /api/auth/register

post.description: Receive and process Stripe webhook events for subscription lifecycle management.
Create a new account with email, username, and password. Returns JWT tokens. A verification email is sent to confirm the address.
Authenticate with email and password. Returns JWT access and refresh tokens. Email must be verified before login is allowed.
Exchange the refresh_token cookie for a new access/refresh token pair. The old refresh token is invalidated (rotation).
Clears httpOnly auth cookies from the browser.
Send a password reset link to the provided email. Always returns success to prevent email enumeration.
Set a new password using the reset token from the email link. Revokes all existing refresh tokens for security.
Confirm email ownership using the verification token sent during registration or email change.

get.description: Redirect the user to GitHub's OAuth authorization page to begin the login flow.
Handle the OAuth callback from GitHub. Sets auth cookies and redirects to the frontend.

post.summary: Request a device verification code for CLI login
post.description: Generate a device code pair for the CLI device authorization flow. The CLI displays the user code and polls for completion.

post.summary: Poll for device code authorization status
post.description: Check whether the user has verified the device code. Returns tokens once verified, or pending/expired status.
Authorize a CLI device by confirming the user code. Requires authentication.
Link a GitHub account to the current authenticated user for OAuth login.

get.description: Return aggregate statistics across all projects the authenticated user is a member of.
Return details of a specific project. The authenticated user must be a member of the project.

put.description: Update project name and/or description. Only owners and editors are allowed to update.

delete.description: Permanently delete a project and all associated data (analyses, environments, variables). Only the project owner can delete.

get.description: Return a paginated list of all members in the project with their roles. Any project member can view the member list.

post.description: Create a pending invitation for a user by email to join the project with a specified role (editor or viewer). If the user is not on the platform, a registration invitation email is sent. Only the project owner can invite members.

put.description: Update the role of a project member. Only the project owner can change member roles.

delete.description: Remove a member from the project. Only the project owner can remove members. The owner cannot be removed.

post.description: Transfer project ownership to another member. The current owner becomes an editor. Only the current owner can transfer ownership.

get.description: Return minimal invitation details (email, project name) for the registration form. No authentication required.

get.summary: List pending invitations for current user
get.description: Return a paginated list of pending project invitations for the authenticated user.

post.description: Accept a pending project invitation using its token. The authenticated user must match the invitation's email.
Decline a pending project invitation using its token. The authenticated user must match the invitation's email.

get.description: Return a paginated list of pending invitations for the project. Any project member can view invitations.

post.description: Resend the invitation email for a pending invitation. Only the project owner can resend invitations.

delete.description: Cancel a pending invitation. Only the project owner can cancel invitations.

put.description: Update a vault group's name, description, or sort order.

delete.description: Delete a vault group and all its environments and variables. This action cannot be undone.
Permanently delete an environment and all its variables. Only owners and editors can delete.

get.description: List environment variables for a project, optionally filtered by environment type. Viewers see masked values; editors and owners see decrypted values.

post.description: Create a new encrypted environment variable for the project. The environment is auto-created if it doesn't exist. Only owners and editors can create variables.

get.description: List the version history for an environment variable. Editors and owners see decrypted values; viewers see masked values.

put.description: Update key, value, or metadata of an environment variable. When the value changes, the previous value is saved as a version snapshot. Only owners and editors can update.

delete.description: Permanently delete an environment variable and all its version history. Only owners and editors can delete.
Permanently delete multiple environment variables and their version history. Only owners and editors can delete.

post.summary: Rollback variable to a previous version
post.description: Restore an environment variable to a previous version. The current value is saved as a new version snapshot before rolling back. Only owners and editors can rollback.

get.description: Compare variable keys across 2-3 environments. Returns rows with match/missing status.

post.description: Sync an environment's variables into another environment. Existing keys are overwritten, new keys are added.

get.description: Generate a .env.example template with keys and placeholder annotations but no real values. Any project member can access this.

post.description: Bulk import pre-encrypted environment variables. The client encrypts values before sending. Existing keys are updated. Only owners and editors can import.

get.description: Export all encrypted environment variables for a given environment. The client is responsible for decryption and formatting. Only owners and editors can export.

post.description: Return encrypted environment variables and secret files for client-side decryption and bundling. Only owners and editors can download bundles.

get.description: Get a template with its variables.

delete.description: Delete a template and all its variables.

post.description: Apply a template to create a new environment with empty-valued variables.

encryptedVariables.description: Pre-encrypted variable entries from the client

get.description: List all version history entries for a secret file. Any project member can view version metadata.
Download an encrypted secret file. Decryption happens client-side. Only owners and editors can download file contents. Viewers can only see metadata via the list endpoint.

put.description: Update the name, description, or vault group of a secret file. Only owners and editors can update.

delete.description: Permanently delete a secret file and all its version history. Only owners and editors can delete.

post.summary: Upload a new version of a secret file
post.description: Replace a secret file's content with a new client-encrypted upload. The current content is saved as a version before being replaced. Only owners and editors can upload new versions.

get.summary: Download a specific version of a secret file
get.description: Download an encrypted previous version of a secret file. Decryption happens client-side. Only owners and editors can download.

post.description: Rollback a secret file to a previous version. The current content is saved as a new version before restoring. Only owners and editors can rollback.

get.description: Returns metadata (type, password-protected, expiry) without consuming the secret.

post.description: Retrieve and permanently consume a shared secret. Content is destroyed after this call — no second access is possible.

post.summary: Create a share link for environment variables
post.description: Generate a one-time shareable link for one or more environment variables. Optional password and expiry supported.

post.summary: Create a share link for a secret file
post.description: Generate a one-time shareable link for a client-encrypted secret file. Optional password and expiry supported.

delete.description: Permanently delete a pending share link. Already-viewed links cannot be revoked.

get.description: Return the authenticated user's profile including email, username, avatar, and linked accounts.

patch.description: Update the authenticated user's username and/or avatar URL. Username must be unique.

delete.description: Permanently delete the authenticated user's account and all associated data including projects, analyses, uploads, and secret files. This action is irreversible.

patch.description: Change the authenticated user's password. Requires the current password for verification. Not available for OAuth-only accounts.
Change the authenticated user's email. Requires password verification. A new verification email is sent to the updated address.

post.description: Upload and set the authenticated user's avatar image. Accepts jpg, png, gif, or webp up to 5 MB.

get.description: Return analysis details with all parsed dependencies. The authenticated user must be a member of the project.

patch.description: Update analysis metadata such as file path. Only owners and editors can update.

delete.description: Delete an analysis and all its dependencies. Only owners and editors can delete analyses.

post.description: Re-check version updates and vulnerabilities for all dependencies in an existing analysis.

get.description: List the authenticated user's GitHub repositories. Requires a linked GitHub account with repo scope.
Scan a GitHub repository for known dependency files (package.json, requirements.txt, etc.). Supports monorepos with nested projects.
Fetch the content of a specific file from a GitHub repository.
Return the number of unread notifications for the authenticated user.

patch.description: Mark all unread notifications as read for the authenticated user.

get.description: Return a single notification by ID.

patch.description: Mark a single notification as read.

get.description: Return a paginated list of secret scans for the project.

post.description: Start a new secret scan for the project's connected GitHub repository. Requires editor or owner role.

get.description: Return details for a specific secret scan.
Return a paginated list of secret detections with optional status and severity filters.

patch.description: Mark multiple detections as resolved or false positive in one request. Only updates OPEN detections. Requires editor or owner role.

get.description: Return scan summary including last scan info, open detections by severity, and resolved count.

patch.description: Mark a detection as resolved or false positive. Requires editor or owner role.

put.description: Update a custom scan pattern. Built-in patterns cannot be modified.

delete.description: Delete a custom scan pattern. Built-in patterns cannot be deleted.

get.description: Download an encrypted secret file using a CI token. Decryption happens client-side using the wrapped DEK.
Return a paginated compliance summary with pass/warn/fail counts for all dependencies in the project.
Export a license audit report as CSV or text.

put.description: Update the policy for an existing license rule.

delete.description: Delete a license policy rule from the project.

get.description: Return aggregated vulnerability and secret scan statistics across all projects the user is a member of.
Return all available subscription plans with pricing and limits.

post.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session for upgrading to a paid plan. Returns a URL to redirect the user to Stripe.
Create a Stripe Customer Portal session for managing billing, invoices, and subscription changes.
Schedule the current subscription to cancel at the end of the billing period. Access continues until then.
Reverse a pending cancellation so the subscription renews normally.

get.description: Check whether the authenticated user has set up their encryption vault.

post.description: Initialize the user's encryption vault with a KEK salt, ECDH keypair, and recovery key hash.

put.description: Update the vault password by providing new wrapped keys and re-wrapped project key grants.

post.description: Recover vault access using the recovery key. Sets a new vault password and re-wraps all keys.

put.description: Generate a new recovery key while the vault is unlocked. Re-wraps all RECOVERY grants.

get.description: Fetch all RECOVERY-type key grants for the current user across all projects.
Fetch all SELF-type key grants for the current user across all projects.
Retrieve another user's ECDH public key for key distribution.
Get the current user's wrapped DEK for this project.
List project members who do not yet have a key grant.

put.description: Update multiple key grants at once (used during vault password change).

get.description: Return aggregate statistics: total users, plan breakdown, MRR, churn.
Return a paginated list of all users with subscription info. Supports search and plan filter.
Return detailed user info with subscription, usage stats, and owned projects.

patch.description: Grant a PRO or TEAM subscription to a user without Stripe charge. Useful for test accounts or internal users.

delete.description: Revert a user's admin-granted subscription back to the FREE plan.

get.description: Return a paginated list of all subscriptions with user info and filters.

post.description: Create a project and add the authenticated user as the owner. Projects serve as containers for dependency analyses and environment variables.

get.description: Return a paginated list of projects where the authenticated user is a member (owner, editor, or viewer).
List all vault groups for a project with environment and variable counts.

post.description: Create a new vault group for organizing environment variables by sub-project or env file.

get.description: List all environments for a project with variable counts.
List all environment templates for a project.

post.description: Create a template from an existing environment or a manual variable list.

get.description: List secret file metadata for a project. File contents are not included — use the download endpoint to retrieve decrypted content.

post.description: Upload a client-encrypted secret file for the project. Executable file types (.exe, .sh, .bat, .cmd, .ps1) are rejected. Max file size is 25 MB. Only owners and editors can upload.

get.description: List all share links created for this project with their status (PENDING, VIEWED, EXPIRED).
Return a paginated list of audit log events for a project. Only owners and editors can view audit logs. Audit logs are append-only and cannot be modified or deleted.
Return a paginated list of analyses for a specific project. The authenticated user must be a member of the project.

post.description: Parse a dependency file and store the analysis results. Accepts file content, ecosystem type, and project ID. Supports Node.js (package.json, package-lock.json) and Python (requirements.txt, pyproject.toml).
Convert configuration content between supported formats (.env, appsettings.json, secrets.yaml, config.toml). No project needed — standalone conversion with preview.

get.description: Return a paginated list of notifications for the authenticated user.
Return built-in and custom scan patterns for the project.

post.description: Create a new custom scan pattern for the project. Requires editor or owner role.

get.description: List all CI/CD tokens for a project.

post.description: Generate a scoped CI/CD token bound to a specific environment.

get.description: Fetch all environment variables and secret file metadata for the token's bound environment.
Return all license policy rules for the project.

post.description: Create a license policy rule mapping an SPDX license ID to ALLOW, WARN, or BLOCK.

get.description: Return a paginated list of audit log events across all projects the user is a member of.
Return the authenticated user's subscription details including plan, limits, and current usage.

post.description: Store a wrapped DEK for a project member.
